model_narrative: This is a long-horizon economic development plan for Pakistan running from the base year 1962 through 1985. Year by year and sector by sector, the plan chooses how much each sector produces, how much is invested and where, how the capital stock builds up, how much the country saves, how much it imports, how much foreign aid it takes in, and how much it consumes. The goal is to maximize total welfare, measured as the present value of consumption over the horizon, less the cost of the foreign aid drawn in, plus a terminal term that credits growth continuing after the plan ends.

sets.name: te
sets.doc: the extended planning years in chronological order from 1962 through 1985; the first member 1962 is the base year that carries the fixed initial conditions, and each later member follows the one before it

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the planning years in chronological order from 1963 through 1985; this is the extended horizon with the base year 1962 removed, so it covers every year that is actually planned

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the production sectors of the economy, one that produces non-traded goods and one that produces traded goods

params.name: sb
params.doc: gross savings in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: tib
params.doc: total investment in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: mb
params.doc: traditional imports in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: gnpb
params.doc: gross national product in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: alpha
params.doc: the marginal savings rate, the fraction of any gain in gross national product above the base year that is added to base-year savings to cap savings

params.name: mgnp
params.doc: the marginal import requirement per unit of gross national product above the base year

params.name: mi
params.doc: the marginal import requirement per unit of total investment above the base year

params.name: p
params.doc: the population growth rate per year, used to set the minimum year-over-year growth of consumption

params.name: beta
params.doc: the maximum allowed year-over-year fractional growth of total investment

params.name: q
params.doc: the aid ratio, the maximum net capital inflow allowed in a year as a fraction of that year's gross national product

params.name: k
params.doc: the capital-output ratio of each sector, the units of capital stock needed per unit of net output; its reciprocal converts capital stock into output capacity

params.name: vb
params.doc: the base-year output capacity of each sector available without any new capital; the non-traded sector starts with the base-year gross national product and the traded sector starts at zero

params.name: e
params.doc: exports in each planning year, in billions of rupees, growing exogenously from the base-year export level

params.name: delt
params.doc: the present-value discount factor applied to each planning year, declining from one as the year moves further into the future

vars.name: gnp
vars.doc: gross national product in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: v
vars.doc: net output of each sector in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: ti
vars.doc: total investment in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: i
vars.doc: investment placed in each sector in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: ks
vars.doc: capital stock of each sector at the start of each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees; it is zero for both sectors in the base year

vars.name: s
vars.doc: gross savings in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: f
vars.doc: net capital inflow, that is foreign aid, in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: fb
vars.doc: the total discounted net capital inflow summed over the planning horizon, in billions of rupees

vars.name: m
vars.doc: traditional imports in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: c
vars.doc: consumption in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees; it is fixed at the base-year level in 1962

vars.name: w
vars.doc: total welfare, the discounted value of consumption net of the cost of foreign aid plus a terminal term for post-horizon growth

description: For each planning year, the gross national product equals the combined net output of all the sectors in that year.

description: For each sector in each planning year, net output cannot exceed the base-year capacity of that sector plus the additional capacity created by its capital stock, where each unit of capital stock yields output in proportion to one over that sector's capital-output ratio.

description: Capital stock accumulates over time. For each sector, the capital stock at the start of the next year equals the capital stock at the start of the current year plus the investment placed in that sector during the current year. This applies for every year that has a following year.
expected_pyomo:
def kbal_rule(model, te, j):
    te_list = list(model.te)
    idx = te_list.index(te)
    if idx < len(te_list) - 1:
        te_next = te_list[idx + 1]
        return model.ks[te_next, j] == model.ks[te, j] + model.i[te, j]
    return Constraint.Skip
model.kbal = Constraint(model.te, model.j, rule=kbal_rule)

model_narrative: This is a long-horizon economic development plan for Pakistan running from the base year 1962 through 1985. Year by year and sector by sector, the plan chooses how much each sector produces, how much is invested and where, how the capital stock builds up, how much the country saves, how much it imports, how much foreign aid it takes in, and how much it consumes. The goal is to maximize total welfare, measured as the present value of consumption over the horizon, less the cost of the foreign aid drawn in, plus a terminal term that credits growth continuing after the plan ends.

sets.name: te
sets.doc: the extended planning years in chronological order from 1962 through 1985; the first member 1962 is the base year that carries the fixed initial conditions, and each later member follows the one before it

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the planning years in chronological order from 1963 through 1985; this is the extended horizon with the base year 1962 removed, so it covers every year that is actually planned

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the production sectors of the economy, one that produces non-traded goods and one that produces traded goods

params.name: sb
params.doc: gross savings in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: tib
params.doc: total investment in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: mb
params.doc: traditional imports in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: gnpb
params.doc: gross national product in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: alpha
params.doc: the marginal savings rate, the fraction of any gain in gross national product above the base year that is added to base-year savings to cap savings

params.name: mgnp
params.doc: the marginal import requirement per unit of gross national product above the base year

params.name: mi
params.doc: the marginal import requirement per unit of total investment above the base year

params.name: p
params.doc: the population growth rate per year, used to set the minimum year-over-year growth of consumption

params.name: beta
params.doc: the maximum allowed year-over-year fractional growth of total investment

params.name: q
params.doc: the aid ratio, the maximum net capital inflow allowed in a year as a fraction of that year's gross national product

params.name: k
params.doc: the capital-output ratio of each sector, the units of capital stock needed per unit of net output; its reciprocal converts capital stock into output capacity

params.name: vb
params.doc: the base-year output capacity of each sector available without any new capital; the non-traded sector starts with the base-year gross national product and the traded sector starts at zero

params.name: e
params.doc: exports in each planning year, in billions of rupees, growing exogenously from the base-year export level

params.name: delt
params.doc: the present-value discount factor applied to each planning year, declining from one as the year moves further into the future

vars.name: gnp
vars.doc: gross national product in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: v
vars.doc: net output of each sector in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: ti
vars.doc: total investment in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: i
vars.doc: investment placed in each sector in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: ks
vars.doc: capital stock of each sector at the start of each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees; it is zero for both sectors in the base year

vars.name: s
vars.doc: gross savings in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: f
vars.doc: net capital inflow, that is foreign aid, in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: fb
vars.doc: the total discounted net capital inflow summed over the planning horizon, in billions of rupees

vars.name: m
vars.doc: traditional imports in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: c
vars.doc: consumption in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees; it is fixed at the base-year level in 1962

vars.name: w
vars.doc: total welfare, the discounted value of consumption net of the cost of foreign aid plus a terminal term for post-horizon growth

description: For each planning year, gross savings cannot exceed base-year savings plus the marginal savings rate times the increase in gross national product over its base-year level.

model_narrative: This is a long-horizon economic development plan for Pakistan running from the base year 1962 through 1985. Year by year and sector by sector, the plan chooses how much each sector produces, how much is invested and where, how the capital stock builds up, how much the country saves, how much it imports, how much foreign aid it takes in, and how much it consumes. The goal is to maximize total welfare, measured as the present value of consumption over the horizon, less the cost of the foreign aid drawn in, plus a terminal term that credits growth continuing after the plan ends.

description: Total investment cannot grow too fast from one year to the next. For each year that has a following year, total investment in the next year cannot exceed the current year's total investment scaled up by one plus the maximum allowed growth rate.
expected_pyomo:
def invu_rule(model, te):
    te_list = list(model.te)
    idx = te_list.index(te)
    if idx < len(te_list) - 1:
        te_next = te_list[idx + 1]
        return model.ti[te_next] <= (1 + model.beta) * model.ti[te]
    return Constraint.Skip
model.invu = Constraint(model.te, rule=invu_rule)

The goal is to maximize total welfare, measured as the present value of consumption over the horizon, less the cost of the foreign aid drawn in, plus a terminal term that credits growth continuing after the plan ends.

expected_pyomo:
def invl_rule(model, te):
    te_list = list(model.te)
    idx = te_list.index(te)
    if idx < len(te_list) - 1:
        te_next = te_list[idx + 1]
        return model.ti[te_next] >= model.ti[te]
    return Constraint.Skip
model.invl = Constraint(model.te, rule=invl_rule)

expected_pyomo:
def conl_rule(model, te):
    te_list = list(model.te)
    idx = te_list.index(te)
    if idx < len(te_list) - 1:
        te_next = te_list[idx + 1]
        return model.c[te_next] >= (1 + model.p) * model.c[te]
    return Constraint.Skip
model.conl = Constraint(model.te, rule=conl_rule)

description: The total discounted net capital inflow equals the sum over all planning years of that year's net capital inflow weighted by its present-value discount factor.

model_narrative: This is a long-horizon economic development plan for Pakistan running from the base year 1962 through 1985. Year by year and sector by sector, the plan chooses how much each sector produces, how much is invested and where, how the capital stock builds up, how much the country saves, how much it imports, how much foreign aid it takes in, and how much it consumes. The goal is to maximize total welfare, measured as the present value of consumption over the horizon, less the cost of the foreign aid drawn in, plus a terminal term that credits growth continuing after the plan ends.

sets.name: te
sets.doc: the extended planning years in chronological order from 1962 through 1985; the first member 1962 is the base year that carries the fixed initial conditions, and each later member follows the one before it

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the planning years in chronological order from 1963 through 1985; this is the extended horizon with the base year 1962 removed, so it covers every year that is actually planned

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the production sectors of the economy, one that produces non-traded goods and one that produces traded goods

params.name: sb
params.doc: gross savings in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: tib
params.doc: total investment in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: mb
params.doc: traditional imports in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: gnpb
params.doc: gross national product in the base year 1962, in billions of rupees

params.name: alpha
params.doc: the marginal savings rate, the fraction of any gain in gross national product above the base year that is added to base-year savings to cap savings

params.name: mgnp
params.doc: the marginal import requirement per unit of gross national product above the base year

params.name: mi
params.doc: the marginal import requirement per unit of total investment above the base year

params.name: p
params.doc: the population growth rate per year, used to set the minimum year-over-year growth of consumption

params.name: beta
params.doc: the maximum allowed year-over-year fractional growth of total investment

params.name: q
params.doc: the aid ratio, the maximum net capital inflow allowed in a year as a fraction of that year's gross national product

params.name: k
params.doc: the capital-output ratio of each sector, the units of capital stock needed per unit of net output; its reciprocal converts capital stock into output capacity

params.name: vb
params.doc: the base-year output capacity of each sector available without any new capital; the non-traded sector starts with the base-year gross national product and the traded sector starts at zero

params.name: e
params.doc: exports in each planning year, in billions of rupees, growing exogenously from the base-year export level

params.name: delt
params.doc: the present-value discount factor applied to each planning year, declining from one as the year moves further into the future

vars.name: gnp
vars.doc: gross national product in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: v
vars.doc: net output of each sector in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: ti
vars.doc: total investment in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: i
vars.doc: investment placed in each sector in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: ks
vars.doc: capital stock of each sector at the start of each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees; it is zero for both sectors in the base year

vars.name: s
vars.doc: gross savings in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: f
vars.doc: net capital inflow, that is foreign aid, in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: fb
vars.doc: the total discounted net capital inflow summed over the planning horizon, in billions of rupees

vars.name: m
vars.doc: traditional imports in each planning year, in billions of rupees

vars.name: c
vars.doc: consumption in each extended-horizon year, in billions of rupees; it is fixed at the base-year level in 1962

vars.name: w
vars.doc: total welfare, the discounted value of consumption net of the cost of foreign aid plus a terminal term for post-horizon growth

description: Welfare equals the discounted sum of consumption over all planning years, minus the cost of foreign capital times the total discounted net capital inflow, plus a terminal term that credits post-horizon growth as a fixed weight times the discounting factor for post-horizon consumption times the gross national product in the final year 1985.

vars.name: w
vars.doc: total welfare, the discounted value of consumption net of the cost of foreign aid plus a terminal term for post-horizon growth

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, for each planning year, the gross national product equals the combined net output of all the sectors in that year. Second, for each planning year, total investment equals gross savings plus the net capital inflow taken in that year. Third, for each extended-horizon year, total investment equals the sum of the investment placed in each sector that year. Fourth, for each planning year, the net capital inflow equals traditional imports minus exports minus the net output of the traded sector. Fifth, for each planning year, gross national product equals consumption plus total investment minus the net capital inflow. Sixth, for each sector in each planning year, net output cannot exceed the base-year capacity of that sector plus the additional capacity created by its capital stock, where each unit of capital stock yields output in proportion to one over that sector's capital-output ratio. Seventh, capital stock accumulates over time. For each sector, the capital stock at the start of the next year equals the capital stock at the start of the current year plus the investment placed in that sector during the current year. This applies for every year that has a following year. Eighth, for each planning year, gross savings cannot exceed base-year savings plus the marginal savings rate times the increase in gross national product over its base-year level. Ninth, for each planning year, traditional imports must be at least the base-year import level plus the marginal import requirement on the increase in gross national product over the base year plus the marginal import requirement on the increase in total investment over the base year. Tenth, total investment cannot grow too fast from one year to the next. For each year that has a following year, total investment in the next year cannot exceed the current year's total investment scaled up by one plus the maximum allowed growth rate. Eleventh, total investment cannot fall from one year to the next. For each year that has a following year, total investment in the next year must be at least the current year's total investment. Twelfth, consumption must keep growing at least with the population. For each year that has a following year, consumption in the next year must be at least the current year's consumption scaled up by one plus the population growth rate. Thirteenth, for each planning year, the net capital inflow cannot exceed the aid ratio times that year's gross national product. Fourteenth, the total discounted net capital inflow equals the sum over all planning years of that year's net capital inflow weighted by its present-value discount factor. Finally, welfare equals the discounted sum of consumption over all planning years, minus the cost of foreign capital times the total discounted net capital inflow, plus a terminal term that credits post-horizon growth as a fixed weight times the discounting factor for post-horizon consumption times the gross national product in the final year 1985.
